purpose: Carry-forward investigation: can the 33 distinct per-ID actions from fanout-#3 actually be applied to n6-arch from this nexus session? Probe accessibility, enumerate actions verbatim, classify safety, default to ZERO writes. OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED is honest.

tier_1_implemented.rationale: Probed /Users/ghost/core/canon: exists, is git repo (branch=main, HEAD=aad6e61d), atlas/atlas.signals.n6 is filesystem-writable (rw-r--r--, 289669 bytes, mtime 2026-04-23). However n6-arch's atlas.signals.n6 is L0 in its own repo per policy — NEVER-edit identical to nexus's L0 protection. Local FS write permission is necessary-but-insufficient for action application; policy gates dominate.

Re-derived 33 distinct per-ID actions from state/signals_sync_per_id_merge_2026-04-27.md and #3's witness. Each enumerated below with verbatim ID + classification + per-action safety verdict. Default outcome: ZERO writes applied this cycle. Reason: (a) atlas.signals.n6 is L0 in n6-arch (cannot edit ANY entry there regardless of additivity), (b) CROSS_PUBLISH 20 are coordination-heavy (cross-repo agreement), (c) APPLY_TRIVIAL 12 modify existing-entry headers + witness counts in n6-arch L0 file (= overwrite of existing facts, NOT pure addition), (d) carry-forward kick listing does not constitute explicit per-action authorization for cross-repo L0 mutation.

facts: F1 n6-arch-locally-accessible: PASS — /Users/ghost/core/canon exists, git repo on branch main, HEAD=aad6e61d, atlas/atlas.signals.n6 fs-writable.
F2 33-actions-precisely-enumerated-with-targets: PASS — see actions[] field below; each entry has sig_id, classification, target_repo, target_file, mutation_kind, safety_verdict.

F3 APPLY_TRIVIAL-12-classification-correct-per-fact: PASS at honest-classification level. The 12 IDs are each HEADER_WITNESS_DRIFT (header gains CROSS tag, witness count +1). Semantically additive in the abstract — but applied in n6-arch they are EDITS of existing @S entries, not pure-additive appends. Therefore not eligible for the 'pure addition' clause of the apply-rule.

F4 CROSS_PUBLISH-20-deferred-with-rationale: PASS — all 20 only-in-nexus signals (SIG-BIG-901..907, 911..917, SIG-CRIT-201, SIG-KURA-201, SIG-META-211, SIG-META-BIG-SUMMARY, SIG-STAR-201, SIG-UNIV-211) are deferred. Rationale: cross-repo SSOT publication requires explicit user/operator coordination (PR or sync-action in n6-arch), not unilateral nexus-side write. Even though they would be APPENDS in n6-arch (new IDs), they touch atlas.signals.n6 which is L0 in that repo.
F5 zero-write-without-explicit-authorization-default: PASS — 0 writes to /Users/ghost/core/canon/** this cycle. 0 writes to /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6. Witness + state markdown only.

F6 atlas.signals.n6-L0-policy: in BOTH nexus AND n6-arch this file is L0 SSOT, NEVER-edit. Kick brief explicitly forbids edit of atlas.signals.n6 / atlas.millennium.n6 in either repo. This dominates over any per-action 'trivial+additive+authorized' allowance.

F7 honesty: OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED is the correct verdict. Inflating 'applied count' by silently writing trivial-looking edits would violate the honesty constraint. The carry-forward kick listing is a research authorization, not a write authorization.
constraints_honored: Cross-repo write default = NO (no explicit user authorization for any of the 33 actions this cycle)
